name: USS Rolf (DE 362)
events: 29 May 1945 HMS Spark (Lt. D.G. Kent, RN) departed Subic Bay for her 5th war patrol (4th in the Far East and 3rd in the South-West Pacific area). She was ordered to patrol to the East of Singapore. This patrol included air/sea rescue duties.
Before proceeding on patrol exercises were carried out with HMS Stygian (Lt. G.S.C. Clarabut, DSO, RN) and USS Rolf (Lt.Cdr. L.E. Hubbell, USNR). (1)

31 May 1945 USS Caiman (Lt.Cdr. W.L. Fey, Jr., USN) made a short call at Subic Bay for fuel and provisions. She departed later the same day for a patrol area to the west of Borneo. She was escorted in and out by USS Rolf (Lt.Cdr. L.E. Hubbell, USNR).
14 Jun 1945 USS Bumper (Cdr. J.W. Williams, Jr., USN) ended her 1st war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines. She was escorted in by USS Rolf (Lt.Cdr. L.E. Hubbell, USNR).
At Subic Bay she was refitted by a relief crew from Submarine Division 261 and USS Anthedon.

19 Aug 1945 At 0500 I/19, USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) joined USS Rasher (T/Lt.Cdr. C.D. Nace, USN), USS Caiman (T/Cdr. W.L. Fey, Jr., USN), USS Carbonero (T/Cdr. C.L. Murphy, USN), USS Woodson (Lt.Cdr. A.R. Corlett, USNR) and USS Rolf (Lt.Cdr. L.E. Hubbell, USNR).
They all arrived at Subic Bay around 0900 I/19.
USS Rasher ending her 8th war patrol.
USS Caiman ending her 4th war patrol.
USS Carbonero ending her 2nd war patrol.
USS Hawkbill ending her 5th war patrol. (2)

21 Aug 1945 Around 0600 I/21, USS Kraken (T/Cdr. T.H. Henry, USN) made rendezvous with USS Puffer (T/Lt.Cdr. C.R. Dwyer, USN), USS Capitaine (T/Lt.Cdr. E.S. Friedrick, USN) and their escort USS Rolf (Lt.Cdr. L.E. Hubbell, USNR). They then proceeded to enter Subic Bay.
At Subic Bay, USS Kraken ended her 4th war patrol. At Subic Bay she was taken in hand for refit by USS Anthedon (T/.Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN).
